value.completed_capture_lifecycle_closure: all 3 completion categories x all 3 derived capture write-out states are validly representable; all 9 pairs require HTTP reconciliation; HTTP_CODE_INVALID persists then stops; non-invalid pairs continue only under the existing 12-cell table
value.http_mapping: total 3x4 state mapping plus equal/unequal valid-status refinement; see table_schemas.http_status_reconciliation.conditional_requirements
value.write_out_evidence_representation: canonical RFC 4648 Base64 raw stdout bytes plus SHA-256, nullable exact ASCII lexeme, derived state, and derived integer

value.hash_rule: complete file SHA-256 plus registered row and logical hashes
value.lifecycle: V5 pre-run; committed after V3 reconciliation and before request manifest

value: COMPLETED capture commit -> HTTP reconciliation row commit -> registered stop or permitted continuation -> compatibility/strict body analysis only when permitted
all three derived write-out evidence states are permitted and explicitly governed for this completion category; no raw stdout byte sequence is excluded

value: body/header/stderr complete and hash-addressed; exact stdout bytes persisted as canonical RFC 4648 Base64 and capture-row-hash-bound
entries ordered by stage_ordinal then unsigned UTF-8 artifact_path
no run_id, run-scoped path, or destination hash field
the V5 inventory includes token_manifest.parquet, request_manifest.parquet, request_plan.parquet, request_plan_hashes.json, and fingerprint readiness exactly once with registered file/logical hashes
created_at_ns is a JSON integer carrying signed UTC nanoseconds; string, decimal, float, exponent, Boolean, and null are invalid
pre_run_attempt_id is excluded from semantic bytes and recomputes from the exact five-field projection
request_plan_url_set_sha256 hashes typed (execution_ordinal,request_id,exact_url_sha256) entries ordered by execution_ordinal
request_execution_order_sha256 hashes typed (execution_ordinal,request_id) entries ordered by execution_ordinal
Every artifact_catalog path is unique and has exactly one schema_ref, one producer, and at least one consumer.
Every table schema defines additional_fields=false, exact fields, key, cardinality, ordering, row hash projection, and logical hash rule.
Every JSON schema defines additional_fields=false, exact fields, required fields, conditional requirements, semantic/file hash rules as applicable.
Every schema ref resolves; every table/json schema has at least one artifact or nested-schema use.
No field uses open state placeholders; nullability is a Boolean field property and structured values use closed schema references.
Every runtime schema uses spec_revision=23; retained embedded contracts keep their original internal revisions and bytes.

REV23 Amendment 03 typed-cell projections are closed for partition_id, token_manifest, request_manifest, request_plan, and http_status_reconciliation; storage type and logical tag are separate, and every nullable null uses NULL
REV23 Amendment 03 has exactly one HTTP reconciliation schema version across table, field domain, row-hash projection, specification, and traceability
REV23 Amendment 03 HTTP write-out raw bytes are canonically Base64-persisted and mechanically determine hash, lexeme, state, and integer for every byte sequence
REV23 Amendment 03 selected-header state and integer are mechanically derived from the retained response-header extraction contract over exact registered evidence

REV23 Amendment 03 completed-capture lifecycle is the total 3 completion categories x 3 derived write-out evidence states; all 9 pairs require HTTP reconciliation before body analysis and invalid always persists then stops
audit_family resolves only through enum:partition_audit_family and never through the broader enum:audit_family
each non-null cell uses its registered nonnull_tag; each nullable null cell uses tag NULL and value null
the typed_cells order equals fields exactly
different semantic bytes under the same ID -> STOP_SEMANTIC_ID_COLLISION
attempt numbers are contiguous per request; each attempt has exactly one STARTED ordinal 0 and at most one terminal ordinal 1
terminal uniqueness: zero terminal means started-only; exactly one terminal is INTERRUPTED or COMPLETED; duplicate/conflicting terminal is structural invalid
every event references the exact authorization file hash and reservation for that request/attempt
effective argv has exactly 19 elements with --disable ordinal 1 and --http1.1 ordinal 2
state-specific one_of contract is total and mutually exclusive
valid INTERRUPTED evidence maps to INTERRUPTED_SELECTED; malformed partial evidence maps to STOP_CAPTURE_EVENT_SCHEMA_INVALID
COMPLETED event evidence is complete and hash-addressed before either analysis row can validate
resume never overwrites an attempt; a new request-specific attempt is max prior attempt + 1
every capture row authorized_request_set_file_sha256 equals the matching authorization-use event and authorization namespace

every capture row authorization_id, authorization_record_file_sha256, authorized_request_set_file_sha256, reservation_id, request_id, execution_ordinal, and attempt_number equal the bound authorization-use values
every COMPLETED capture persists canonical Base64 for the exact stdout bytes; decoding and re-encoding must reproduce the stored string exactly

COMPLETED http_code_evidence_state and http_status_code are mechanically derived from exact decoded stdout bytes; invalid evidence is HTTP_CODE_INVALID with null integer and is not rejected before HTTP reconciliation evidence is persisted

each COMPLETED curl completion category EXIT_0, EXIT_28_TIMEOUT, and EXIT_OTHER_NONZERO permits exactly the three mechanically derived evidence states HTTP_CODE_000, HTTP_CODE_100_599, and HTTP_CODE_INVALID

HTTP_CODE_INVALID remains a validly represented COMPLETED capture state until exactly one HTTP reconciliation row is persisted; that row then produces STOP_HTTP_WRITEOUT_INVALID before compatibility or strict body analysis
no COMPLETED capture routes directly to C02, C03, or C07-C23; every completed category/state pair passes through HTTP reconciliation first
only non-invalid write-out evidence may be evaluated by the existing selected-header reconciliation and compatibility precedence

value: For every COMPLETED category and every derived HTTP_CODE_000, HTTP_CODE_100_599, or HTTP_CODE_INVALID state, persist exactly one table:http_status_reconciliation row before compatibility or strict body analysis. HTTP_CODE_INVALID maps to write_out_http_code_state INVALID and STOP_HTTP_WRITEOUT_INVALID after that row is persisted; non-invalid states may continue only according to the registered 12-combination reconciliation table.

value.binding: field is included in capture_event_row_sha256 and is copied byte-equivalently into HTTP reconciliation write_out_stdout_raw_base64
value.encoding: canonical RFC 4648 Base64 with standard alphabet and required padding; empty byte sequence is empty string

value: No COMPLETED capture routes directly to C02, C03, or C07-C23. The HTTP reconciliation row is mandatory first. Only a non-invalid write-out state whose registered HTTP/header reconciliation cell permits continuation may enter compatibility precedence; strict body analysis is permitted only where the same reconciliation result permits it. Missing analysis later yields COMPLETED_UNANALYZED.

Decode canonical RFC 4648 Base64 from http_status_stdout_raw_base64 to exact bytes B. If B is ASCII 000, http_code_evidence_state=HTTP_CODE_000 and http_status_code=null. If B is exactly three ASCII digits whose decimal value is 100..599, state=HTTP_CODE_100_599 and http_status_code equals that value. Every other byte sequence, including three ASCII digits outside those ranges and malformed length/content, maps to HTTP_CODE_INVALID with http_status_code=null. No raw byte sequence is unrepresentable.

value.invalid_rule: HTTP_CODE_INVALID -> reconciliation INVALID -> NOT_RECONCILABLE_STOP + HTTP_WRITEOUT_INVALID + STOP_HTTP_WRITEOUT_INVALID before compatibility or strict body analysis
value.noninvalid_rule: HTTP_CODE_000 and HTTP_CODE_100_599 proceed only through selected-header derivation and the registered 12-combination reconciliation table

classification_order.predicate: complete registered header bytes are unavailable, oversize, unreadable, or hash-mismatched
retained parser output selected_http_status_code is non-null
parser selected_http_status_code is null and ordered_header_issue_codes contains HEADER_NO_FINAL_RESPONSE_BLOCK
parser selected_http_status_code is null and ordered_header_issue_codes contains HEADER_STATUS_LINE_INVALID
parser selected_http_status_code is null and neither preceding registered parser issue applies

value.parser_output_binding: selected_header_status_state and selected_header_status_int equal this ordered adapter output; independently supplied values cannot override it
source_binding: capture_completed_event_row_sha256 resolves uniquely to the selected COMPLETED capture row
header_evidence_sha256 equals that capture row header_sha256
the exact complete bytes at the registered header_path hash to header_evidence_sha256; truncated, oversize, unreadable, missing, or hash-mismatched evidence is not parser input

classification_order.predicate: len(B) != 3 OR any byte is outside 0x30..0x39
len(B)=3, all ASCII digits, and decimal(B) in 100..599
len(B)=3, all ASCII digits, and decimal(B) in 001..099 or 600..999

counterexamples.reason: integer must equal decimal lexeme and registered raw bytes
200 maps only to VALID_STATUS with integer 200
three-digit out-of-range evidence maps to INVALID with null integer
two raw bytes map to INVALID with null lexeme and integer
four raw bytes map to INVALID with null lexeme and integer
ASCII letter O makes the evidence malformed; expected lexeme and integer are null
Unicode digits are not three ASCII digit bytes; expected lexeme and integer are null
non-ASCII bytes map to INVALID with null lexeme and integer
lexeme must be decoded from registered bytes without normalization

source_binding.canonical_base64: decode with standard RFC 4648 Base64; reject nonalphabet, misplaced padding, omitted required padding, or any stored lexeme whose decode-then-reencode differs; empty string represents empty bytes
source_binding.capture_row: capture_completed_event_row_sha256 resolves uniquely to the selected COMPLETED capture row
source_binding.raw_base64_equality: write_out_stdout_raw_base64 equals that capture row http_status_stdout_raw_base64 byte-for-byte as a UTF-8 Base64 lexeme
source_binding.stdout_hash: stdout_evidence_sha256 = lowercase SHA-256 of the decoded raw stdout bytes

value.totality: cases are ordered, total, and mutually exclusive over every finite byte sequence

totality_proof.conclusion: exactly one classification_order case applies to every finite byte sequence
totality_proof.digit_value_partition: for exactly three ASCII digits, decimal(B) is exactly one member of the disjoint exhaustive ranges {0}, 1..99, 100..599, or 600..999
totality_proof.first_partition: exactly one of: len(B) != 3 or a byte is non-ASCII-digit; otherwise B is exactly three ASCII digits

OTHER_UNEQUAL.predicate: write_out_http_status_int != selected_header_status_int AND pair not in {(200,404),(404,200)}

value: conditional_requirements has exactly the 12 Cartesian pairs of 3 write-out states and 4 selected-header states; no wildcard is permitted
VALID_STATUS write-out requires write_out_http_status_int non-null in 100..599; VALID_000 and INVALID require it null
VALID_STATUS selected header requires selected_header_status_int non-null in 100..599; every other selected-header state requires it null
VALID_STATUS/VALID_STATUS subcases are total and mutually exclusive: equal, 200/404, 404/200, and other unequal
issue_code is null only for equal VALID_STATUS/VALID_STATUS evidence
only equal VALID_STATUS/VALID_STATUS can reach C14-C23; no-selected-final-header branches are limited to C01-C13 front-end continuation
all structural mismatch and invalid write-out branches stop before C14-C23
every row-hash null cell uses logical tag NULL and value null
schema_version equals exactly local_curl_http_status_reconciliation.v23; no alias or alternate HTTP reconciliation schema version is accepted
write_out_stdout_raw_base64 is canonical Base64 copied exactly from the bound COMPLETED capture row; stdout_evidence_sha256 hashes its decoded bytes
write_out lexeme, integer, and state are outputs of write_out_evidence_derivation and cannot be independent caller assertions
write_out_http_code_lexeme is non-null exactly for every three-ASCII-digit byte sequence, including INVALID out-of-range values; it is null for malformed evidence
selected_header_status_state and selected_header_status_int equal the retained response-header parser adapter output over exact hash-verified registered header bytes
VALID_STATUS selected header has integer 100..599; every other selected-header state has null integer
conditional_requirements remains the exact 12 Cartesian pairs and is applied only after write-out and header evidence derivation
every selected COMPLETED category/state pair persists exactly one HTTP reconciliation row before any compatibility or strict body analysis
HTTP_CODE_INVALID capture state maps to reconciliation state INVALID and always produces STOP_HTTP_WRITEOUT_INVALID after the reconciliation row is persisted
request_id, execution_ordinal, and (condition_id,outcome_index) are each unique
referenced token row exists, its hash recomputes, query_eligible=true, invalid_window_state=QUERY_ELIGIBLE, and invalid_window_reason is null
all copied fields are typed-equal to the referenced token row
request_id recomputes from the registered seven-field typed identity
token_id alias, coercion, fallback, and normalization are forbidden
encode destination-excluding typed rows; order by execution_ordinal numeric; join with one LF and no trailing LF; SHA-256
exact one-to-one membership with request manifest
all copied values are typed-equal to the matched request-manifest row
exact URL serializer output and exact URL/policy/transport/subprocess hashes are required
encode destination-excluding typed rows; order by execution_ordinal numeric; join with one LF and no trailing LF; SHA-256
exactly 300 distinct condition_id values and exactly outcome_index {0,1} per condition
source_d_token_row_sha256 resolves uniquely in the same pre-run attempt and recomputes from table:source_d_token
source_b_source_d_reconciliation_row_sha256 resolves uniquely in the same pre-run attempt and recomputes from table:source_b_source_d_reconciliation
both referenced rows have the same typed key and overall_reconciliation_state=RECONCILED
all copied and derived values are typed-equal to governing Source D and accepted reconciliation values
query eligibility derives only from accepted reconciliation; source presence alone cannot establish eligibility
token_id_lexeme is the exact original reconciled lexeme, including retained .0, and is never winner-derived
encode each registered typed row projection; order by condition_id unsigned UTF-8 then outcome_index numeric; join with one LF and no trailing LF; SHA-256; exclude row-hash destination field

UINT8, UINT16, UINT32, and UINT64 storage hash as UINT; INT64 storage hashes as INT; UTC nanoseconds hash as UTC_NS; STRING, BOOL, SHA256, and STRING_LIST retain those logical tags; storage width never creates a width-specific logical tag
